note: The honors calculus courses 31AH-BH-CH are unusually demanding and are intended for students with strong aptitude and deep interest in Mathematics. For more information on the sequence, go to Honors Calculus Please contact mathadvising@math.ucsd.edu if you have any questions.

title: Mathematical Reasoning
note: Math majors: You can review upper division course offerings for the current academic year HERE . Waitlisted math majors will have priority to enroll in MATH 109 over non-math majors.

subject: MATH

note: Students who completed Math 18 will need to submit an EASy request to enroll in Math 174 to clear the Linear Algebra prerequisite

subject: MATH
title: Introduction to Probability
note: Completion of or concurrent enrollment in MATH 109 is strongly recommended for MATH 180A. Two units of credit offered for MATH 180A if ECON 120A taken previously, no credit offered for MATH 180A if ECON 120A is taken concurrently. Students will earn two units of credit offered for MATH 180A if MATH 183 or 186 taken previously or concurrently.

subject: MATH
title: Enumerative Combinatorics
note: Please note that CSE 20 and/or 21 are not equivalent to Math 109 for prerequisite purposes for Math 184.

subject: MATH
title: Industry Practicum
note: The Math 197 internship is intended for students who have found a paid or unpaid position off-campus (in a math-related field) and would like to earn academic credit for the work. Students seeking academic credit for their position should sign up for the listed Math 197 section under Professor Kedlaya. Students looking to work with a different faculty supervisor should instead enroll in Math 199.

subject: MATH
title: Independent Study/Undergrad
note: If you are interested in a Math 199, your first step would be to think about which faculty member/research area you would like to work with and then ask them if they are willing to act as your advisor. Students should generally have completed two quarters of upper division coursework in the mathematical area in which they plan to work. A good resource for matching faculty and research areas is the Math Department website: http://www.math.ucsd.edu/research/all-research-areas/

subject: MATH
title: Honors/Thesis Resrch/Undergrad
note: MATH 199H students should go to the Mathematics Advising Office, APM 7409, to obtain information about the Mathematics Honors Program.
